title: potentilla caulescens subsp. nivalis
intro: Potentilla caulescens subsp. nivalis, also known as Snow Cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its white or pink flowers and edible roots. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Potentilla caulescens subsp. nivalis are said to include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
